item.title: Introducing the Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader Launcher
item.description:
Lord Captains!

We’re introducing Owlcat Launcher to Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader! The launcher is designed to serve as a hub, helping you stay up to date with the latest news while getting into the game as quickly as ever. Have no worries – your adventures, saves, mods, and the game itself remain unchanged. The launcher will just make news, updates and games’ content more accessible and easier to find.

Our launcher is still on its way to the final form – we plan to continue developing it, adding new features and improvements designed to make your experience with our games more convenient and enjoyable.

To help you get to know Owlcat Launcher better and address any questions you may have, we've prepared this article. Let's get started!

What is the Owlcat Launcher?

Owlcat Launcher is a new launch interface with news and information about all our games.

We always do our best to be open and honest with you, so here are a few disclaimers before we move on:
  • We are aware that launchers as a thing could be clumsy and annoying, and how people often react to certain products of this kind. We want to be transparent with you and are open to any feedback you’re willing to share.
  • We want to make this tool as unobtrusive as possible, and also to provide a lot of options and flexibility for the users.
  • We want our solution to be lightweight, require no mandatory registration and be useful and beneficial not only for us, but also for our players.
  • The tool is produced completely in-house, it doesn’t use or install any external software, and it does not collect any data from your computer.

Why do we need a Launcher?

With so much happening (both within our own ecosystem and across an increasingly crowded market) we noticed it's becoming more challenging for players to keep up with news and updates about our games. And for us, as a growing studio with multiple new unannounced RPGs already in production, being able to reach our players is crucial.

For example, according to our research, a surprisingly large portion of the Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous audience who express a clear desire to play a ‘similar CRPG’ have never even heard of Rogue Trader. This, upon reflection, is understandable—in this age of information overload, it's incredibly easy to miss even news that might be of interest. Therefore, we felt we really needed an effective cross-promotion tool for all our titles.

Thus, the idea of Owlcat Launcher was born.


How can it be useful to me?

Owlcat Launcher serves as a central hub for everything related to our games. Instead of searching across multiple platforms for updates, news, and announcements, now you can find them all gathered in one convenient place whenever you launch the game. What can be done in our launcher:
  • See a list of all our games, information about them and links to their social media pages all in one place;
  • See the news and important announcements for all our current and upcoming games;
  • You can choose to create an Owlcat Account. It will allow you to access all our portals with a single login;
  • Purchase, install or launch any of our games regardless of platform.
But we want to make the launcher even more useful and expand its features even further, including:
  • Connecting your existing founder website accounts to the launcher account and receiving the beta/release keys directly through the launcher;
  • Accessing additional content like the digital OSTs, artbooks, and in-game founder rewards of our games directly through the launcher;
  • Signing up to community events and giveaways directly through the launcher;
We’re planning to gradually evolve the launcher over time with more tools and QoL features. Speaking of which, what features or tools would make your experience with our games better? Let us know what you think – this customizable platform opens up numerous possibilities!


What if I don’t want to use it?

You’re free not to!

While we’re making sure that the launcher is as seamless and lightweight as possible and does not bother you with mandatory registration or any other similar annoyances, we understand that some people may still simply not be interested.

That is no problem – if you do not wish to see our news or use any of the services provided by the launcher, you can switch it off in the settings and launch the game directly. It will continue to operate just fine without the launcher.


That’s it! Please don't forget to share your thoughts with us in the comments. Good luck on your adventures across the Koronus Expanse and beyond, Lord Captain.
item.content:encoded:
Lord Captains!

We’re introducing Owlcat Launcher to Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader! The launcher is designed to serve as a hub, helping you stay up to date with the latest news while getting into the game as quickly as ever. Have no worries – your adventures, saves, mods, and the game itself remain unchanged. The launcher will just make news, updates and games’ content more accessible and easier to find.

Our launcher is still on its way to the final form – we plan to continue developing it, adding new features and improvements designed to make your experience with our games more convenient and enjoyable.

To help you get to know Owlcat Launcher better and address any questions you may have, we've prepared this article. Let's get started!

What is the Owlcat Launcher?

Owlcat Launcher is a new launch interface with news and information about all our games.

We always do our best to be open and honest with you, so here are a few disclaimers before we move on:
  • We are aware that launchers as a thing could be clumsy and annoying, and how people often react to certain products of this kind. We want to be transparent with you and are open to any feedback you’re willing to share.
  • We want to make this tool as unobtrusive as possible, and also to provide a lot of options and flexibility for the users.
  • We want our solution to be lightweight, require no mandatory registration and be useful and beneficial not only for us, but also for our players.
  • The tool is produced completely in-house, it doesn’t use or install any external software, and it does not collect any data from your computer.

Why do we need a Launcher?

With so much happening (both within our own ecosystem and across an increasingly crowded market) we noticed it's becoming more challenging for players to keep up with news and updates about our games. And for us, as a growing studio with multiple new unannounced RPGs already in production, being able to reach our players is crucial.

For example, according to our research, a surprisingly large portion of the Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous audience who express a clear desire to play a ‘similar CRPG’ have never even heard of Rogue Trader. This, upon reflection, is understandable—in this age of information overload, it's incredibly easy to miss even news that might be of interest. Therefore, we felt we really needed an effective cross-promotion tool for all our titles.

Thus, the idea of Owlcat Launcher was born.


How can it be useful to me?

Owlcat Launcher serves as a central hub for everything related to our games. Instead of searching across multiple platforms for updates, news, and announcements, now you can find them all gathered in one convenient place whenever you launch the game. What can be done in our launcher:
  • See a list of all our games, information about them and links to their social media pages all in one place;
  • See the news and important announcements for all our current and upcoming games;
  • You can choose to create an Owlcat Account. It will allow you to access all our portals with a single login;
  • Purchase, install or launch any of our games regardless of platform.
But we want to make the launcher even more useful and expand its features even further, including:
  • Connecting your existing founder website accounts to the launcher account and receiving the beta/release keys directly through the launcher;
  • Accessing additional content like the digital OSTs, artbooks, and in-game founder rewards of our games directly through the launcher;
  • Signing up to community events and giveaways directly through the launcher;
We’re planning to gradually evolve the launcher over time with more tools and QoL features. Speaking of which, what features or tools would make your experience with our games better? Let us know what you think – this customizable platform opens up numerous possibilities!


What if I don’t want to use it?

You’re free not to!

While we’re making sure that the launcher is as seamless and lightweight as possible and does not bother you with mandatory registration or any other similar annoyances, we understand that some people may still simply not be interested.

That is no problem – if you do not wish to see our news or use any of the services provided by the launcher, you can switch it off in the settings and launch the game directly. It will continue to operate just fine without the launcher.


That’s it! Please don't forget to share your thoughts with us in the comments. Good luck on your adventures across the Koronus Expanse and beyond, Lord Captain.

item.title: Feature Highlight - Skitarii Weapons
item.description:
Rising above the shackles of the flesh, the Skitarii have been rebuilt with the divine augments of the Adeptus Mechanicus to best serve the Omnissiah, as an impossibly efficient tool of war.

In case you missed it, check out the reveal trailer here.

This is part of an ongoing series of feature highlights focused on the upcoming DLC class: The Skitarii.

In our previous highlight we talked in depth about the design of the class itself and its talent tree. This feature highlight will focus on the arsenal available to Skitarii in Darktide, exploring the design philosophy behind the unique weapons and talking about what existing weapons they have access to. We’ve interviewed our Game Designers Erik and Matteo for this!

“I’m Matteo, I’m a Game Designer specializing in Weapons. My favourite class is Psyker, and I make a sick tiramisú.” ~ Matteo

“I’m Erik, a Game Designer also specializing in Weapons. My favourite classes are Zealot and Psyker. I can confirm that Matteo makes a sick tiramisú. I can't cook or bake but I'll make you a perfect 'Last word' cocktail.” ~ Erik

Weapons Design

As always, the first thing that went into the development of the Skitarii’s weapons was sitting down and choosing what options from the source material’s arsenal to include. The Adeptus Mechanicus has a very rich and varied set of weapons that are unique to them, many of which are specifically Skitarii weapons, too. So there were definitely a lot of possibilities to choose from, especially on the ranged side.

“So we needed to say, okay, if we have to choose a subset of this, what are the ones that we say are unique, diverse, and capture the gameplay fantasy of the Skitarii the most.” ~ Matteo

“We both look at not just what’s available in the lore - what they actually use in the tabletop and in the universe - but also what type of gameplay slots we’re trying to fill, what weapons we have on the existing classes, what type of role we want this class to have...” ~ Erik

After deciding on these options, the design team goes through several iterations of implementing ideas, trying them out, seeing what works and what doesn’t, and so on. This is especially true of the Arc weapons, which use and enable a brand new mechanic unique to this class, the Arc mechanic.

If you missed our explanation of how the Arc mechanic works for Arc weapons and talents, check out the previous Feature Highlight here!

At the same time, a couple of the non-Arc weapons coming with the Skitarii exist in a similar thematic space as some of the existing weapons in Darktide, and so we took great care into carving a unique gameplay space for them so that they stand out even from those.

For all weapons, the Weapon Designers worked closely with the Class Designers to make sure that the class’ abilities and its weapons synergized and supported each other well.

With all that said, let’s take a look at the brand new weapons, which will be exclusive to the Skitarii class.

New Weapons
Arc Maul

The Arc Maul is a powerful blunt weapon that can electrocute enemies and trigger the Arc mechanic. Since other classes already have access to similar weapons in the form of Shock Mauls, we wanted to make sure that this weapon felt and played very differently from those.

Normal attacks will electrocute the enemies. Hitting enemies also starts building up charges for the weapon’s special attack, up to a maximum of eight.

Activating the weapon special will switch to an amped up state where attacks will drain charges and trigger an Arc effect with each attack until you either run out of charges or manually deactivate the mode.

“So the loop is that you hit enemies to electrocute them and accumulate charges, then you activate the weapon and hit them again to trigger an Arc.” ~ Matteo

Beyond its special mechanics, the Arc Maul is a relatively safe weapon with strong stagger and crowd control properties, but it relies on the Arc mechanic for most of its damage.

As an added bonus, this weapon deals more damage with all attacks when hitting a target that is already electrocuted.

Paired Transonic Blades

Our second dual-wield melee weapon for Darktide, the Paired Transonic Blades are composed of a short Transonic Razor in the off hand and a longer Transonic Blade in the main hand.

Using the Weapon Special, this weapon can switch between two ‘attunements’, which give the weapon two different movesets, each specialized to deal with different types of enemies.

“This makes the weapon very versatile, and probably one of the most complex weapons we have in Darktide.” ~Matteo

The default moveset is attuned to most effectively deal with unarmoured enemies, featuring wide, sweeping attacks that can easily cleave and mow through hordes. The second attunement specializes in eliminating armoured enemies, and the moveset switches to a more single target, high damage set of stabs and vertical attacks that easily pierce through Flak and Carapace.

While you can switch between modes when idle, pressing the Weapon Special in the middle of an attack chain will allow you to perform a unique attack as part of a transition into the other mode, letting you switch seamlessly in the middle of combat.

“It’s a weapon that players can immediately play with; you don’t need to study hours to understand it. But the more you play with it, the more you find different ways to approach the situation in a more efficient manner. It’s also a really cool looking weapon, hehehe.” ~ Matteo

Mechanicus Power Sword

Power weapons are a very iconic melee choice for Adeptus Mechanicus as a whole, and so we definitely wanted to give the Skitarii their own version of this weapon. To differentiate them from the existing Darktide Power Swords in gameplay, we leaned into the fact that the Skitarii’s version is much better maintained and looked after than the requisitioned ones our other classes have access to.

As such, the Mechanicus Power Swords are considered to always be in an active, low power mode and are a powerful weapon even without using their Weapon Special. The weapon will start with 6 charges, which can be spent by pressing the Weapon Special to perform a single, high-powered powered attack (a tap will perform a light attack, while holding the button will perform a heavy attack). Each Special Attack consumes one charge, which recharges over time.

However, holding onto these charges also provides a passive buff to the weapon’s normal attacks, which is the source of the weapon’s power.

“So the idea is that the player can say ‘Okay, I’m not using the charges so the weapon remains at maximum strength because it has all the stacks’ or ‘I’ll spend the charges to immediately get a strong attack but momentarily reduce the strength of the weapon’.” ~ Matteo

The Special Attacks are empowered versions of the normal Mechanicus Power Sword moveset, sharing animations and chained actions. Said moveset is also very different from the existing Power Swords, featuring a bigger focus on thrusts and stabs.

Galvanic Rifle

The Galvanic Rifle is a powerful, semi-automatic precision rifle that fills a niche that hadn’t been covered in Darktide’s arsenal yet. Each shot deals relatively high damage and can do a good job at piercing through armour, although it has a low ammo count.

“I wouldn’t call it a sniper, but definitely a precision weapon.” ~ Erik

While you can run and hipfire it with decent accuracy (although with strong recoil), the weapon excels when aiming down sights and landing weak spot hits, such as headshots. It is a simple, but elegant weapon that feels extremely satisfying to shoot with.

“It behaves like you would expect it to after looking at it, and I think we got it to an interesting gameplay spot as well. It’s a simple design, but extremely effective!.” ~ Erik

Arc Rifle

The Skitarii Alpha Primus’ Arc Rifle in Darktide is a high ammo, heavy, slow firing ranged weapon that shoots bolts of lightning. Unlike the Psyker’s Smite Blitz, these are not sustained streams of electricity but rather quick, instant bolts that hit their target and dissipate. While it has a high ammo capacity, reloading it takes longer than most weapons, so be careful not to waste your shots!

“With the other Skitarii weapons being relatively low ammunition, we wanted to make the Arc Rifle like a slow firing heavy machine gun, but with Arc properties.” ~ Erik

Each shot will apply an electricity effect like other shock or Arc weapons, and when hitting an already electrocuted enemy it will Arc to a nearby enemy (which will also electrocute it, or Arc again if it’s already electrocuted!). This means that it takes prolonged, sustained fire on a horde of enemies to really build up the damage as the electricity spreads, but becomes unstoppable once it’s properly enabled.

Phosphor Blast Pistol

“It’s a gigantic hand cannon is what it is.” ~ Erik

The Phosphor Blast Pistol is a powerful and unique ranged weapon. While it has a limited ammo capacity and isn’t all that accurate, it excels in both damage and support. Each shot of hot-white phosphor blasts deals high damage to the target and then splashes Phosphor to enemies nearby.

“It does a fair amount of damage, it’s not weak by any means, but the support utility is what makes it super interesting.” ~ Erik

Both the initial target and the enemies in the splash zone will suffer a powerful debuff from the lingering Phosphor compound, which makes them not just take more damage from all sources, but attacks will also cleave through them more easily.

“It’s got a super cool effect where you can see it actually burns pieces of the armour off and exposes the flesh on some enemies.” ~ Erik

Beyond looking gnarly, this effect also helps communicate to your teammates which enemies are debuffed by the Phosphor Pistol, encouraging them to take advantage of the situation.

Existing Weapons
Plasma Gun Mk III

On top of all of these brand new weapons that we’re adding for the Skitarii class, this update will also see the release of a new mark of the Plasma Gun! Available to both the Veteran and Skitarii classes, this Plasma Gun variant will have less focus on penetrating through enemies and terrain, and more on devastating explosions, which will trigger when hitting most enemy types.

Beyond that, the Skitarii class will also have access to the following weapons that can already be found in Darktide’s arsenal:

Ranged Weapons

  • Infantry Autogun  

  • Braced Autogun  

  • Vigilant Autogun  

  • Heavy Laspistol  

  • Plasma Gun Mk II

  • Quickdraw Stub Revolver  

  • Shredder Autopistol

Melee Weapons

  • Assault Chainsword  

  • “Devil’s Claw” Sword  

  • Shock Maul

  • Power Falchion  

  • Combat Blade


Bonus: Which of the new weapons is your favorite?

“The Dual Transonics! It’s a design idea that I’ve had for a while, it kinda builds upon the Hive Scum’s Crowbar and Bone Saw \[...] It’s basically two movesets in one weapon, and I think it turned out extremely nice, so yeah.” ~ Matteo

“I’m gonna say the Galvanic Rifle and the Arc Maul. Ping and bonk.” ~ Erik

That’s all we have for today! We hope you enjoyed this early look at the Skitarii class’ weapons!

The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now. 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/Praise the Omnissiah!

We’ll see you on the Mourningstar.
item.content:encoded:
Rising above the shackles of the flesh, the Skitarii have been rebuilt with the divine augments of the Adeptus Mechanicus to best serve the Omnissiah, as an impossibly efficient tool of war.

In case you missed it, check out the reveal trailer here.

This is part of an ongoing series of feature highlights focused on the upcoming DLC class: The Skitarii.

In our previous highlight we talked in depth about the design of the class itself and its talent tree. This feature highlight will focus on the arsenal available to Skitarii in Darktide, exploring the design philosophy behind the unique weapons and talking about what existing weapons they have access to. We’ve interviewed our Game Designers Erik and Matteo for this!

“I’m Matteo, I’m a Game Designer specializing in Weapons. My favourite class is Psyker, and I make a sick tiramisú.” ~ Matteo

“I’m Erik, a Game Designer also specializing in Weapons. My favourite classes are Zealot and Psyker. I can confirm that Matteo makes a sick tiramisú. I can't cook or bake but I'll make you a perfect 'Last word' cocktail.” ~ Erik

Weapons Design

As always, the first thing that went into the development of the Skitarii’s weapons was sitting down and choosing what options from the source material’s arsenal to include. The Adeptus Mechanicus has a very rich and varied set of weapons that are unique to them, many of which are specifically Skitarii weapons, too. So there were definitely a lot of possibilities to choose from, especially on the ranged side.

“So we needed to say, okay, if we have to choose a subset of this, what are the ones that we say are unique, diverse, and capture the gameplay fantasy of the Skitarii the most.” ~ Matteo

“We both look at not just what’s available in the lore - what they actually use in the tabletop and in the universe - but also what type of gameplay slots we’re trying to fill, what weapons we have on the existing classes, what type of role we want this class to have...” ~ Erik

After deciding on these options, the design team goes through several iterations of implementing ideas, trying them out, seeing what works and what doesn’t, and so on. This is especially true of the Arc weapons, which use and enable a brand new mechanic unique to this class, the Arc mechanic.

If you missed our explanation of how the Arc mechanic works for Arc weapons and talents, check out the previous Feature Highlight here!

At the same time, a couple of the non-Arc weapons coming with the Skitarii exist in a similar thematic space as some of the existing weapons in Darktide, and so we took great care into carving a unique gameplay space for them so that they stand out even from those.

For all weapons, the Weapon Designers worked closely with the Class Designers to make sure that the class’ abilities and its weapons synergized and supported each other well.

With all that said, let’s take a look at the brand new weapons, which will be exclusive to the Skitarii class.

New Weapons
Arc Maul

The Arc Maul is a powerful blunt weapon that can electrocute enemies and trigger the Arc mechanic. Since other classes already have access to similar weapons in the form of Shock Mauls, we wanted to make sure that this weapon felt and played very differently from those.

Normal attacks will electrocute the enemies. Hitting enemies also starts building up charges for the weapon’s special attack, up to a maximum of eight.

Activating the weapon special will switch to an amped up state where attacks will drain charges and trigger an Arc effect with each attack until you either run out of charges or manually deactivate the mode.

“So the loop is that you hit enemies to electrocute them and accumulate charges, then you activate the weapon and hit them again to trigger an Arc.” ~ Matteo

Beyond its special mechanics, the Arc Maul is a relatively safe weapon with strong stagger and crowd control properties, but it relies on the Arc mechanic for most of its damage.

As an added bonus, this weapon deals more damage with all attacks when hitting a target that is already electrocuted.

Paired Transonic Blades

Our second dual-wield melee weapon for Darktide, the Paired Transonic Blades are composed of a short Transonic Razor in the off hand and a longer Transonic Blade in the main hand.

Using the Weapon Special, this weapon can switch between two ‘attunements’, which give the weapon two different movesets, each specialized to deal with different types of enemies.

“This makes the weapon very versatile, and probably one of the most complex weapons we have in Darktide.” ~Matteo

The default moveset is attuned to most effectively deal with unarmoured enemies, featuring wide, sweeping attacks that can easily cleave and mow through hordes. The second attunement specializes in eliminating armoured enemies, and the moveset switches to a more single target, high damage set of stabs and vertical attacks that easily pierce through Flak and Carapace.

While you can switch between modes when idle, pressing the Weapon Special in the middle of an attack chain will allow you to perform a unique attack as part of a transition into the other mode, letting you switch seamlessly in the middle of combat.

“It’s a weapon that players can immediately play with; you don’t need to study hours to understand it. But the more you play with it, the more you find different ways to approach the situation in a more efficient manner. It’s also a really cool looking weapon, hehehe.” ~ Matteo

Mechanicus Power Sword

Power weapons are a very iconic melee choice for Adeptus Mechanicus as a whole, and so we definitely wanted to give the Skitarii their own version of this weapon. To differentiate them from the existing Darktide Power Swords in gameplay, we leaned into the fact that the Skitarii’s version is much better maintained and looked after than the requisitioned ones our other classes have access to.

As such, the Mechanicus Power Swords are considered to always be in an active, low power mode and are a powerful weapon even without using their Weapon Special. The weapon will start with 6 charges, which can be spent by pressing the Weapon Special to perform a single, high-powered powered attack (a tap will perform a light attack, while holding the button will perform a heavy attack). Each Special Attack consumes one charge, which recharges over time.

However, holding onto these charges also provides a passive buff to the weapon’s normal attacks, which is the source of the weapon’s power.

“So the idea is that the player can say ‘Okay, I’m not using the charges so the weapon remains at maximum strength because it has all the stacks’ or ‘I’ll spend the charges to immediately get a strong attack but momentarily reduce the strength of the weapon’.” ~ Matteo

The Special Attacks are empowered versions of the normal Mechanicus Power Sword moveset, sharing animations and chained actions. Said moveset is also very different from the existing Power Swords, featuring a bigger focus on thrusts and stabs.

Galvanic Rifle

The Galvanic Rifle is a powerful, semi-automatic precision rifle that fills a niche that hadn’t been covered in Darktide’s arsenal yet. Each shot deals relatively high damage and can do a good job at piercing through armour, although it has a low ammo count.

“I wouldn’t call it a sniper, but definitely a precision weapon.” ~ Erik

While you can run and hipfire it with decent accuracy (although with strong recoil), the weapon excels when aiming down sights and landing weak spot hits, such as headshots. It is a simple, but elegant weapon that feels extremely satisfying to shoot with.

“It behaves like you would expect it to after looking at it, and I think we got it to an interesting gameplay spot as well. It’s a simple design, but extremely effective!.” ~ Erik

Arc Rifle

The Skitarii Alpha Primus’ Arc Rifle in Darktide is a high ammo, heavy, slow firing ranged weapon that shoots bolts of lightning. Unlike the Psyker’s Smite Blitz, these are not sustained streams of electricity but rather quick, instant bolts that hit their target and dissipate. While it has a high ammo capacity, reloading it takes longer than most weapons, so be careful not to waste your shots!

“With the other Skitarii weapons being relatively low ammunition, we wanted to make the Arc Rifle like a slow firing heavy machine gun, but with Arc properties.” ~ Erik

Each shot will apply an electricity effect like other shock or Arc weapons, and when hitting an already electrocuted enemy it will Arc to a nearby enemy (which will also electrocute it, or Arc again if it’s already electrocuted!). This means that it takes prolonged, sustained fire on a horde of enemies to really build up the damage as the electricity spreads, but becomes unstoppable once it’s properly enabled.

Phosphor Blast Pistol

“It’s a gigantic hand cannon is what it is.” ~ Erik

The Phosphor Blast Pistol is a powerful and unique ranged weapon. While it has a limited ammo capacity and isn’t all that accurate, it excels in both damage and support. Each shot of hot-white phosphor blasts deals high damage to the target and then splashes Phosphor to enemies nearby.

“It does a fair amount of damage, it’s not weak by any means, but the support utility is what makes it super interesting.” ~ Erik

Both the initial target and the enemies in the splash zone will suffer a powerful debuff from the lingering Phosphor compound, which makes them not just take more damage from all sources, but attacks will also cleave through them more easily.

“It’s got a super cool effect where you can see it actually burns pieces of the armour off and exposes the flesh on some enemies.” ~ Erik

Beyond looking gnarly, this effect also helps communicate to your teammates which enemies are debuffed by the Phosphor Pistol, encouraging them to take advantage of the situation.

Existing Weapons
Plasma Gun Mk III

On top of all of these brand new weapons that we’re adding for the Skitarii class, this update will also see the release of a new mark of the Plasma Gun! Available to both the Veteran and Skitarii classes, this Plasma Gun variant will have less focus on penetrating through enemies and terrain, and more on devastating explosions, which will trigger when hitting most enemy types.

Beyond that, the Skitarii class will also have access to the following weapons that can already be found in Darktide’s arsenal:

Ranged Weapons

  • Infantry Autogun  

  • Braced Autogun  

  • Vigilant Autogun  

  • Heavy Laspistol  

  • Plasma Gun Mk II

  • Quickdraw Stub Revolver  

  • Shredder Autopistol

Melee Weapons

  • Assault Chainsword  

  • “Devil’s Claw” Sword  

  • Shock Maul

  • Power Falchion  

  • Combat Blade


Bonus: Which of the new weapons is your favorite?

“The Dual Transonics! It’s a design idea that I’ve had for a while, it kinda builds upon the Hive Scum’s Crowbar and Bone Saw \[...] It’s basically two movesets in one weapon, and I think it turned out extremely nice, so yeah.” ~ Matteo

“I’m gonna say the Galvanic Rifle and the Arc Maul. Ping and bonk.” ~ Erik

That’s all we have for today! We hope you enjoyed this early look at the Skitarii class’ weapons!

The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now. 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/Praise the Omnissiah!

We’ll see you on the Mourningstar.

item.title: Hotfix 1.6.0.482 is live!
item.description:
A new hotfix 1.6.0.482 is live! A few more issues were fixed:

- Fixed the broken Self-Knowledge dialogue with Eogunn. Players who already experienced this issue will receive Eogunn back into their party.
- Fixed a broken cutscene at the end of Kiava Gamma with the Daemon Engine;
- Fixed multiple cases with familiars disappearing. If you already lost your familiar, simply come to the bridge to get it back.
- Removed party members icons from the middle of the screen during cutscenes;
- Fixed multiple co-op desyncs on locations with elevators;
item.content:encoded:
A new hotfix 1.6.0.482 is live! A few more issues were fixed:

- Fixed the broken Self-Knowledge dialogue with Eogunn. Players who already experienced this issue will receive Eogunn back into their party.
- Fixed a broken cutscene at the end of Kiava Gamma with the Daemon Engine;
- Fixed multiple cases with familiars disappearing. If you already lost your familiar, simply come to the bridge to get it back.
- Removed party members icons from the middle of the screen during cutscenes;
- Fixed multiple co-op desyncs on locations with elevators;

item.title: Hotfix 1.6.0.475 is live!
item.description:
Lord Captains!

Yet another hotfix 1.6.0.475 is on the way, fixing two major bugs:

  • Inability to jump into the vent during the Void Shadows storyline - fixed;
  • It was possible to gain access to Act V systems via Trazyn's Museion, completely breaking the game - fixed;

We are delivering this hotfix quickly to stop the Act V problem from appearing again, but reverting the progress on existing saves requires more research and will come in other upcoming updates. If you had this issue in your playthrough, for now please load an earlier save.

We are sorry for the inconvenience.

As usual, consoles will get the update with up to 48 hours delay.
item.content:encoded:
Lord Captains!

Yet another hotfix 1.6.0.475 is on the way, fixing two major bugs:

  • Inability to jump into the vent during the Void Shadows storyline - fixed;
  • It was possible to gain access to Act V systems via Trazyn's Museion, completely breaking the game - fixed;

We are delivering this hotfix quickly to stop the Act V problem from appearing again, but reverting the progress on existing saves requires more research and will come in other upcoming updates. If you had this issue in your playthrough, for now please load an earlier save.

We are sorry for the inconvenience.

As usual, consoles will get the update with up to 48 hours delay.

item.title: Connection / desync issues - Looking into it
item.description:
UPDATE - 19/06

We just put out a patch in preparation for another one coming soon that will have proper fixes. Testing is going well already

Here's the patch notes for today's update:

  • Prepped networking backend for upcoming patch

  • Re-enabled host migration

  • Ping measurement and region determination should be more accurate now

Excited to see how this next patch lands as it should make playing the game a hell of a lot more enjoyable ːsteamthumbsupː

OI GITS!

We've seen a bunch more players jump on now that the EGS version was released yesterday - which is great

What's not-so-great is that unfortunately we've also seen reports of connection issues, with host/client desync and matches ending suddenly, kicking everyone back to the menu, as well as other things

We're looking into this as top priority and hope to have some fixes ASAP

In the meantime, here's some potential workarounds Can't join a game:

  • Try a different one for a bit then try the previous one again if you still want to join it

Can't join any games / can't create a game:

  • Restart Steam. Make sure Speed Freeks isn't running still in Task Manager

  • If that fails, restart your PC

Crashed or kicked back to menu while hosting, or general bad performance while hosting

  • Make sure there's no heavy processes running in the background

  • If you have medium to low end PC, you perhaps shouldn't be hosting as it requires more processing power than joining a hosted game

If any of these don't work, we'll have to wait for the patch then

We're sorry for the connection problems and appreciate your patience while we try to fix them

Hopefully you'z can still get sum krumpin' dun in da meantime though

We'll update you as soon as we have progress
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE - 19/06

We just put out a patch in preparation for another one coming soon that will have proper fixes. Testing is going well already

Here's the patch notes for today's update:

  • Prepped networking backend for upcoming patch

  • Re-enabled host migration

  • Ping measurement and region determination should be more accurate now

Excited to see how this next patch lands as it should make playing the game a hell of a lot more enjoyable ːsteamthumbsupː

OI GITS!

We've seen a bunch more players jump on now that the EGS version was released yesterday - which is great

What's not-so-great is that unfortunately we've also seen reports of connection issues, with host/client desync and matches ending suddenly, kicking everyone back to the menu, as well as other things

We're looking into this as top priority and hope to have some fixes ASAP

In the meantime, here's some potential workarounds Can't join a game:

  • Try a different one for a bit then try the previous one again if you still want to join it

Can't join any games / can't create a game:

  • Restart Steam. Make sure Speed Freeks isn't running still in Task Manager

  • If that fails, restart your PC

Crashed or kicked back to menu while hosting, or general bad performance while hosting

  • Make sure there's no heavy processes running in the background

  • If you have medium to low end PC, you perhaps shouldn't be hosting as it requires more processing power than joining a hosted game

If any of these don't work, we'll have to wait for the patch then

We're sorry for the connection problems and appreciate your patience while we try to fix them

Hopefully you'z can still get sum krumpin' dun in da meantime though

We'll update you as soon as we have progress
